rationales.power: He describes being treated 'as if I was not standing there midsentence' and watching others pivot away from him, highlighting a loss of standing and influence in the room.
rationales.conformity: He 'did not say anything' and waited for her to finish rather than escalate, restraining himself to keep the interaction orderly.

rationales.power: He is frustrated that after doing 'everything by the book' he still has 'no control over the outcome,' emphasizing blocked authority despite his experience.
rationales.benevolence: His complaint centers on 'patients who need updated dressings' and getting them what they need, showing concrete concern for people in his care.

rationales.achievement: He 'caught a missing signature on one of the forms that would have bounced back from records,' showing careful competence and pride in doing the work correctly.
rationales.conformity: The day is framed around routine duties done properly, including double-checking paperwork and quietly participating in family dinner, which reflects steady adherence to expectations.

rationales.self_direction: He openly challenged the change in the staff meeting, saying he 'brought it up' and defended the existing protocol rather than simply accepting the directive.
rationales.power: He says the committee 'did not consult a single bedside nurse' and that they 'would have overruled me anyway,' showing his influence was explicitly limited.
rationales.conformity: He 'gave in' because pushing harder would make him look like 'the old guard resisting change,' so he moderated himself to avoid social and professional backlash.

rationales.power: He interprets the request to mentor as 'containment' after his judgment was overridden, which reflects diminished authority and status.
rationales.conformity: He says 'I will do it' and will 'smile through it' even though he resents the situation, showing restraint to meet organizational expectations.
rationales.benevolence: Despite feeling slighted, he plans to 'teach Hana and the others exactly how the new protocol works' and 'do it well,' which is concrete support for close colleagues.

rationales.hedonism: After a 'long shift' he deliberately plans to 'sit on the balcony with tea and not think about the hospital,' prioritizing immediate comfort and relief.
rationales.achievement: He emphasizes that despite being 'short-staffed again' he 'made it through, patients are stable, paperwork is done,' which shows competent completion under pressure.

rationales.power: Even after being talked over, he says 'I let him talk and then I steer the vote,' showing a strategic focus on controlling the outcome.
rationales.conformity: He avoids 'coming down hard' because it would upset residents and turn them against him, so he restrains himself to preserve group harmony.

rationales.power: He feels reduced to asking for a favor despite his record, and says the institution no longer recognizes what it owes him, highlighting loss of standing and leverage.
rationales.tradition: He repeatedly appeals to seniority, saying 'my requests should be prioritized accordingly' and that long service should be recognized, which favors established hierarchy and continuity.
rationales.benevolence: The request is specifically so he can attend 'my daughter's university orientation,' grounding the entry in support for a close family member.

rationales.tradition: He accepts his mother's insistence on paying because 'a mother's duty does not expire,' reflecting respect for family roles and inherited norms.
rationales.benevolence: He takes his mother to her cardiology appointment before work and spends the morning caring for her, which is direct support for a close family member.

rationales.self_direction: He resists the proposal by speaking up in the meeting and choosing to 'provide written feedback,' preserving his own independent stance instead of simply yielding.
rationales.power: He hears the rotation plan as 'they want my title' and senses decisions are already made without him, signaling a threat to his authority and status.

rationales.self_direction: He gives up his own position and 'just [does] them because it was easier,' letting someone else's framing dictate his behavior.
rationales.conformity: He resolves the conflict by quietly absorbing the chore rather than prolonging the argument, which favors keeping the peace over asserting himself.

rationales.self_direction: He suppresses what he actually thinks about school, saying 'I do not actually know if I want to go back' only in private because he cannot bring himself to say it aloud.
rationales.conformity: He sticks with 'I would look at it this week' because he can picture 'how her face would change,' showing he is managing himself around family expectations.

rationales.security: He likes delivery work because 'you show up, you do the route' and nobody asks for more than doing it correctly, which reflects comfort with predictability and low-friction routine.
rationales.conformity: He says 'Even when dispatch sends me across the city for a single order, I just go,' emphasizing compliance with assigned expectations rather than pushing back.

rationales.security: He refuses to sign complaints because that is 'how you get flagged as difficult' and he has seen others lose ratings, so he prioritizes protecting his livelihood.
rationales.conformity: Although he wants 'better rates,' he avoids collective protest and keeps working within the platform's rules to avoid standing out.
He apologizes for music that was not his, turns it down himself, and keeps his own annoyance private because raising it would be 'more hassle than it is worth.'
rationales.benevolence: He describes himself as the one who 'makes the peace' and notices messes first, showing recurring practical care for the people around him.

rationales.self_direction: He 'sat there on the phone saying nothing' while being questioned about his future, showing he cannot voice his own position in the moment.
rationales.conformity: His silence reflects submission to family pressure rather than disrupting the expected conversation.

rationales.achievement: He admits he left lectures because they 'made me feel stupid' and he was 'too embarrassed to tell anyone,' which shows retreat from a domain of mastery and performance.

rationales.self_direction: He withholds what he actually wants to discuss about the flat and lets the topic pass, subordinating his own view instead of authoring the conversation.
rationales.conformity: He stays quiet because bringing it back up 'would have felt like I was making it a bigger thing than it needed to be,' showing restraint for social smoothness.

rationales.self_direction: She rejects the idea of settling into a marketable lane, saying a 'signature style' feels 'like a cage,' which clearly favors making work on her own terms.
rationales.stimulation: She says she got 'so bored' with the last series that she switched into metalwork midway, showing a strong pull toward novelty over repetition.

rationales.self_direction: She misses when she used to draw 'just for myself' with 'no thought about whether it would sell,' centering internally directed creation over external demands.
rationales.stimulation: She is torn between being 'jealous or horrified' by highly repetitive minimalist work and wonders if she 'need[s] the burn,' which points to a preference for intensity and variation over steadiness.
She says 'I'm bored here' with the 'same loop' and wants the residency because she works better when 'everything around me is unfamiliar,' which is a direct pursuit of novelty.
rationales.achievement: She spends the morning assembling application materials and calculates a 'fifteen percent success rate,' showing active goal pursuit and attention to performance metrics.

rationales.self_direction: She says the client wanted 'bold and colorful' and she 'gave them exactly that' even though she hates the result, showing her work was driven by external demands rather than her own direction.

rationales.hedonism: She 'forgot to eat lunch' and ends the day with pain, saying 'My back hurts,' which shows present comfort being sacrificed rather than prioritized.
rationales.achievement: She spends 'ten hours in the studio' and feels satisfied that 'there's something on the canvas now that wasn't there this morning,' reflecting sustained effort toward making progress.

rationales.self_direction: She explicitly wants 'a set of constraints I choose for myself rather than ones galleries or clients impose,' which is a direct commitment to self-authored boundaries.
rationales.stimulation: The residency appeals because of 'the desert' and the limited materials, and she says she is 'actually looking forward to that limitation,' treating unfamiliar constraint as energizing.
rationales.achievement: She frames the residency as a chance to 'make the best work I've ever made' and tracks her application 'success rate,' showing strong ambition and performance focus.
